citation.anchor: All registers beyond register CPUID[3].number are reserved and raise a Reserved Register/Field fault if they are accessed

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled and serialized
The common General Exception handler advances CR.IIP by one bundle and executes RFI
inputs: Every low selector from zero through 255
expected_results: CPUID0 through CPUID4 equal the declared synthetic profile
Both high-bit selectors alias their low-eight-bit fixed register
Fault count is 251, mismatch count is zero, and the terminal bundle is reached
allowed_results: The nonarchitectural diagnostic exception record may retain the final delivered General Exception after architectural RFI completion
permitted_state_changes: Test-owned selector, fault, and mismatch registers
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
expected_faults: Reserved Register/Field for every selector CPUID5 through CPUID255
cases.committed_prefix: Each fixed read commits once; each reserved read commits only the handler counters before RFI continues at the next bundle.
cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, add 16 to CR.IIP, write CR.IIP, and execute RFI for each of 251 faults.

oracle.derivation: The fixed values, selector boundary, ignored high bits, ISR code, and 251-fault count are test-owned literals derived from the cited CPUID register layouts and indirect-register access rule.

limitations: CPL1 through CPL3 equivalence, false qualification, and selector NaT priority are separate closure units
